title: [Bug]: Metrics CLI describe command fails strict CI type checks
summary: The release CI matrix reported a strict type-checking and linting issue in the metrics CLI describe path.
observed: The GitHub Actions CI run failed during `mypy src` with a redundant cast in `src/nats_sinks/cli/metrics.py`; after the cast was removed locally, Ruff correctly identified the now-unused import.
expected: The metrics CLI should pass Ruff and mypy in all release-matrix Python versions without suppressing useful static-analysis feedback.
reproduction: Run `mypy src` and `ruff check src/nats_sinks/cli/metrics.py` against the release candidate commit.
failing_test: The release CI matrix provided the failing check. The focused local guard is `mypy src` plus `ruff check src/nats_sinks/cli/metrics.py`.
impact: Release automation could not complete while strict type checking failed, even though runtime metrics behavior was otherwise unchanged.
delivery_semantics: The fix is limited to CLI typing and lint hygiene. It does not change NATS delivery, sink writes, ACK ordering, or metrics values.
security: The fix does not expose secrets, does not change payload logging, and keeps the metrics CLI dependency-free.
acceptance: The redundant metrics CLI cast is removed.
The focused metrics CLI tests pass.
Ruff and mypy pass for the affected source file and source tree.
tests: Run `ruff check src/nats_sinks/cli/metrics.py tests/unit/test_release_checksums.py`, `mypy src`, and `pytest tests/unit/test_metrics_cli.py tests/unit/test_public_api.py tests/unit/test_release_checksums.py -q`.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md so release notes explain the CI repair.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the CI fix has been published and the GitHub Release evidence is available.
